title: Divide by 8
strategies: Halve three times (Г·8 = Г·2 Г·2 Г·2).

learn.title: Sharing Into 8 Equal Groups
learn.content: When you divide by 8, you share a total equally among 8 groups. Example: 48 counters shared into 8 groups в†’ each group gets 6. So 48 Г· 8 = 6.

learn.title: Halve Three Times Strategy
learn.content:
Dividing by 8 is the same as halving three times (because 8 = 2Г—2Г—2). Example: 24 Г· 8
Step 1: 24 Г· 2 = 12
Step 2: 12 Г· 2 = 6
Step 3: 6 Г· 2 = 3
Result: 24 Г· 8 = 3. Check: 8 Г— 3 = 24 вњ“

learn.title: Array: 8 Rows
learn.content: A 8Г—9 array has 72 tiles arranged in 8 rows of 9. Reading it as division: 72 tiles in 8 rows means 72 Г· 8 = 9 tiles per row. The Г—8 fact 8Г—9=72 gives the Г·8 fact 72Г·8=9.

learn.title: Number Line: Backward Jumps of 8
learn.content: To find 72 Г· 8, start at 72 on a number line and hop backward by 8 each time: 72 в†’ 64 в†’ 56 в†’ 48 в†’ 40 в†’ 32 в†’ 24 в†’ 16 в†’ 8 в†’ 0. Count the hops: 9 hops. So 72 Г· 8 = 9.

learn.title: The Г·8 Pattern: Reverse the Г—8 Fact

try.explanation: 8 Г· 8 = 1. Any number divided by itself is 1.
hints: Use the 8Г— fact: 8 Г— ? = 8. Only 8 Г— 1 = 8.
How many groups of 8 fit inside 8? Just one.

feedback.incorrect: 8 Г· 8 means how many groups of 8 in 8. Just one: 8 Г· 8 = 1.

Try halving three times: 48Г·2=24, 24Г·2=12, 12Г·2=6.

feedback.correct: Yes! 8 Г— 6 = 48, so there are 6 octopuses.
feedback.incorrect: Halve 48 three times: 48Г·2=24, 24Г·2=12, 12Г·2=6. So 48 Г· 8 = 6 octopuses.

practice_r2.explanation: 64 Г· 8 = 8, not 7. All other facts are correct.

For 64 Г· 8: halve three times вЂ” 64Г·2=32, 32Г·2=16, 16Г·2=8. So 64 Г· 8 = 8, not 7.

feedback.correct: Correct! 64 Г· 8 = 8, not 7. Halve three times: 64Г·2=32, 32Г·2=16, 16Г·2=8.
feedback.incorrect: Check 64 Г· 8: 8Г—8=64, so 64Г·8=8. The claim of 7 is wrong.
